rulings.Case Title: DAVID ROBERTS VS. ASSOCIATED INSULATION OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Temporary Plant Cleaners, Inc.S Notice Of Its Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 5, 2015 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Temporary Plant Cleaners, Inc.'s motion for judgment on the pleadings: hearing required. The hearing will be at 9:45a.m. Both sides must bring proposed orders to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT BIDINOST et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 5, 2015 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant Goodman Lumber Company's motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. As to summary judgment, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that decedent Robert Bidinost was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories and the interrogatory responses of United States Gypsum Company preclude defendant from sustaining its burden. Even if defendant had sustained its burden, the deposition testimony of decedent and Howard Bowman and the interrogatory responses of United State Gypsum Company would create a triable issue whether decedent was exposed to asbestos-containing joint compound attributable to defendant. As to summary adjudication of plaintiffs' causes of action for negligence and strict liability, defendant failed to sustain its burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence supporting their contention that defendant owed a duty to decedent. Defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar because it did not demonstrate that plaintiffs failed to provide meaningful responses to comprehensive written discovery designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiffs have to support their claim that defendant owed a duty to decedent. Additionally defendant failed to sustain its burden of negating the duty element of plaintiffs' claims. Plaintiffs' objections to Charles Goodman's deposition testimony are sustained. However even if it was admissible, it would still not negate the duty element. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT BIDINOST et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 5, 2015 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant Frederick Meiswinkel, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment or, in the alternative, summary adjudication is denied as untimely. Defendant failed to provide proper notice pursuant to C.C.P. sections 437c(a), 1010.6, 12a and 12c. Plaintiffs' objection based on timeliness is sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: SHARON MCCLARY et al VS. CROWN CORK & SEAL COMPANY, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 5, 2015 in Department 503, Line 4. Defendant Therma Corporation's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that defendant did not owe decedent any legal duty and thus failed to negate that element of plaintiffs' claims. Defendant also failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant owed a duty to decedent. Defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar because it did not demonstrate that plaintiffs failed to provide meaningful responses to comprehensive written discovery designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiffs have to support their claim that defendant owed a duty to decedent. Specifically, while there is no dispute that plaintiffs' allegations against defendant are limited to take-home/secondary exposure, defendant failed to demonstrate facts that require the conclusion that plaintiffs' claims fall within the holding of Campbell v. Ford Motor Company (2012) 206 Cal.App.4th15. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
